Article.ArticleTitle: Operational Research on Operational Research: Assessing the Impact of the Structured Operational Research and Training Initiative on Tackling Antimicrobial Resistance in Ghana.

Abstract.AbstractText: "If research is to have impact and improve outcomes, it must be locally relevant and the findings actionable to shape policy and/or practice [...].

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: UNICEF/UNDP/World Bank/WHO Special Programme on Research and Training in Tropical Diseases (TDR), 20, Avenue Appia, 27, 1211 Geneva, Switzerland.
International Union Against Tuberculosis and Lung Disease, 2 Rue Jean Lantier, 75001 Paris, France.
Ghana Country Office, World Health Organization, 12 Agbaamo St, Accra P.O. Box 142, Ghana.
Antimicrobial Resistance Department, World Health Organization, 20, Avenue Appia, 27, 1211 Geneva, Switzerland.
International Union Against Tuberculosis and Lung Disease, 2 Rue Jean Lantier, 75001 Paris, France.
Department of Clinical Research, Faculty of Infectious and Tropical Diseases, London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine, Keppel Street, London WC1E 7HT, UK.
UNICEF/UNDP/World Bank/WHO Special Programme on Research and Training in Tropical Diseases (TDR), 20, Avenue Appia, 27, 1211 Geneva, Switzerland.
National Antimicrobial Resistance Policy Platform, Accra P.O. Box 44, Ghana.
Department of Pharmacy Practice, School of Pharmacy, University of Health and Allied Sciences, Ho P.O. Box 31, Ghana.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: The authors declare no conflicts of interest. The authors alone are responsible for the views expressed in this article and they do not necessarily represent the views, decisions or policies of the institutions with which they are affiliated.
